diagnosis: ECG findings are left atrial abnormality(LAA) (biphasic P in V1 with broad negative component) and broad P waves elsewhere plus a tall R in V1 with a vertical QRS axis (about +80 degrees). A tall R in V1 with a vertical or particularly rightward axis generally indicates right ventricular hypertrophy (RVH). The combination of LAA with evidence of RVH in a dyspneic patient is most c/w with rheumatic mitral valve disease with severe mitral stenosis (MS). That's what this patient had with mild mitral regurgitation (MR) as well.

report.intervals: PR: 160 ms, QRS: 80 ms, QTc: 447 ms
report.findings: Left atrial abnormality (LAA) evidenced by broad, notched P waves in limb leads and a biphasic P wave in V1 with a prominent negative terminal component; Right ventricular hypertrophy (RVH) evidenced by a tall R wave in V1 and vertical axis; ST-T wave inversions in leads V1-V3 likely representing RV strain.
report.impression: Left atrial abnormality and right ventricular hypertrophy in a patient with rheumatic mitral valve disease (severe mitral stenosis and mild mitral regurgitation).
